content.text: Groups & Scenes

The EP-133 organizes sounds and patterns into 4 groups (A, B, C, D). Each group has 12 sounds and up to 99 patterns. Groups play simultaneously, allowing you to layer drums, bass, melody, and effects.
Groups
Press A, B, C, or D to select that group. The selected group determines which sounds the pads trigger and which pattern is edited. All four groups play back together, each with their own pattern.

content.caption: Group selection buttons A, B, C, D

content.title: Suggested Group Organization
content.text: A common setup: Group A for drums, Group B for bass, Group C for chords/pads, Group D for melody/FX. But use whatever organization works for your workflow!
Group Contents
Each group contains:

items: Fader assignment (what parameter the fader controls)

content.text: Muting Groups
Mute or unmute a group by pressing SHIFT + the group button (A, B, C, or D). Muted groups are silenced but continue to play internally - unmuting brings them back in time.

content.title: Performance Technique
content.text: Use group muting for live performance: mute the drums for a breakdown, then bring them back for impact.
Patterns
Each group can store up to 99 patterns. Use +/- to switch between patterns in the current group. Enter a pattern number directly with the number keys and ENTER.

items: Pattern 1-99 per group (396 patterns total per project)
Pattern changes can be queued during playback

content.text: Scenes
A scene is a snapshot of which pattern is active in each group. Scenes allow you to switch all four group patterns simultaneously, creating arrangement sections like verse, chorus, and bridge.
Scenes are used in Song Mode to arrange your composition. Each song position specifies a scene to play.
Selecting Patterns
To select a pattern for the current group:

items: Make sure you're in the main view (press MAIN if needed)
Select the group you want to change (A, B, C, or D)
Use +/- to scroll through patterns, or
Enter a pattern number with number keys and press ENTER
The new pattern starts at the next bar (queued change)

content.text: Copying Patterns
Copy a pattern to another slot to create variations. This preserves your original while letting you experiment with changes.
Projects
The EP-133 stores 9 projects. Each project contains all 4 groups with their sounds, patterns, and song arrangement. Switch projects with MAIN + number key (1-9).

content.title: Save Before Switching
content.text: Commit your current project (hold MAIN/COMMIT) before switching to another project to avoid losing work.
